title: Yellow Level 2 warning: Wind and waves for the Western Cape
description: GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The South African...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The South African Weather Service has issued a Yellow Level 2 warning for wind and waves along parts of the Western Cape coastline.


> The warning is in effect from 12:00 SAST on Monday, 15 September, until 24:00 SAST on Tuesday, 16 September.


A cold front is expected to pass south of the country on Monday, followed by a high-pressure system.

This will bring strong to gale-force westerly to south-westerly winds of 50 to 70 km/h, gusting up to 80–100 km/h, between Cape Point and Cape Agulhas from Monday afternoon.

By Monday evening, conditions will extend eastwards to Plettenberg Bay, continuing into Tuesday.

Winds will be accompanied by south-westerly waves of 4.0 to 6.0 metres, resulting in very rough and choppy seas.

Impacts:

- Hazardous navigation at sea, with small vessels and personal watercraft such as kayaks at risk of capsizing.
- Localised disruption to beachfront activities, as well as to small harbours and ports, is possible for short periods.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
